rationale: Nunes (2000) is cited directly in the theory section, not only in a generic citation cluster, to justify a key premise behind the proposed convenience-salience framework. That premise helps position why consumption incidence can be triggered at the point of consumption rather than fully determined at purchase. The citation is not used for method, data, or direct model extension, but it plays a meaningful framing role in the paper's conceptual setup.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Nunes (2000) as conceptual support for the claim that consumption occasions are often not fully anticipated, which motivates its salience-based account of consumption incidence. It also references Nunes when identifying consumption-frequency forecasting as a future antecedent of postpurchase consumption behavior.
